methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $8.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Endurosat is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $167M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies, including Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace, and Loft Orbital.

Two quantitative methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $200M total-raised proxy with a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, yields $340M. Peer-median analysis across the 5 same-stage, same-sector private comparables produces $1.1B, with a range from $1.0B (Isar Aerospace, Loft Orbital) to $8.0B (Sierra Space). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 42.9x across the sector) cannot be applied absent disclosed target revenue.

The triangulated valuation range is $289M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $745M across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion—particularly the $8.0B Sierra Space outlier—reflects material heterogeneity in capital intensity, revenue stage, and market position within the Series C cohort. Key uncertainties include Endurosat's revenue run-rate relative to peers, customer concentration, and path to profitability. A refined estimate would benefit from disclosure of current annual revenue and recurring revenue mix.
